title: Trevia inophyllum
intro: Trevia nudiflora, also known as the "Trevia" or "Tropical Tamarind", is a tropical tree species native to the tropical forests of Asia. The plant is valued for its medicinal properties, which include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and exhibit antioxidant and anti-diabetic activities, making it a promising natural remedy for various health conditions. The bioactive constituents of Trevia include flavonoids, alkaloids, and phenolic compounds, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. Trevia is commonly used in traditional medicine and herbal preparations, such as teas, infusions, and extracts, which are used to treat digestive issues, fever, and other ailments.
taxonomy: Trevia inophyllum is a plant species commonly known as the 'Sea Mango' or 'Achacha'. Trevia inophyllum is classified under the family Rutaceae, which includes a large group of flowering plants commonly known as the citrus or rue family. This plant belongs to the genus Trevia, a small group of flowering plants native to tropical regions. It is a small evergreen tree or shrub that grows up to 10 meters in height. Trevia inophyllum is native to tropical regions of the Americas and the Caribbean.
uses: Trevia inophyllum, also known as the Beach Morning Glory, has been utilized in traditional medicine for its various health benefits. Its leaves and other parts have been used to treat fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions such as eczema and dermatitis. The plant is also believed to have anti-inflammatory properties, which may help in reducing swelling and pain. In addition, Trevia inophyllum has been used to treat respiratory issues, including coughs and colds. Its medicinal properties are attributed to the presence of various compounds, including flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects.
